name: Fig Newtons # 3
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the shortening and 1 cup of sugar until smooth.
2. Add the beaten egg and beat until light and fluffy.
3. Mix together the milk and vanilla in a separate bowl.
4. In a separate bowl, sift together the salt, flour, and baking powder.
5. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the creamed mixture, alternating with the milk, until well combined.
6. Roll out the dough on a lightly floured surface to an 1/8 inch thickness in a rectangle shape.
7. Place the chopped figs in a saucepan with the remaining sugar and 1 cup of boiling water. Cook for 5 minutes, then let cool.
8. Spread the cooled fig mixture over half of the dough.
9. Cover with the remaining half of the dough.
10. Cut into oblong bars.
11. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 12-15 minutes.
(Note: This recipe was passed down to me from my grandmother many years ago and has always turned out well.)
